fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.fonds_title: Vaux family fonds (shared with George Vaux Jr. and Mary Vaux)
fonds.scope: Photography, glacier observations, correspondence, publications. Includes photographic sequences from 1897-1913 of Illecillewaet, Yoho, and Asulkan glaciers. Lantern slides, photographs, postcards, prints, negatives.
fonds.findings: Found 260 photographs dated 1906 in V653. William S. Vaux Jr. was co-photographer and glacier observer with his brother George Jr. Series V653/I/Y contains nitrate negatives from 1906-1909. Series V653/PA contains 114 prints (1897-1907) of mountain landscape, railway, and glacier views. Multiple series with Yoho Pass, Emerald Lake, and glacier monitoring materials.
fonds.notes: William S. Vaux Jr. (1872-1908) was the younger brother. Died at age 36, limiting the extent of his documented work to the period before his death. The 1906 Yoho camp attendance represents a significant documented expedition during his lifetime. Together with George Jr., William participated in establishing repeat photography protocols for glacier monitoring.
sample_catalogues: V653/PS-3 (hand-coloured postcard, 1900-1906, hand-coloured by sister Mary M. Vaux)
V653/PA-1 to PA-114 (prints, 1897-1907, glacier and mountain views)

leads_pending_manual_access.reason: Quaker family archives; potential biographical and family correspondence materials
Vaux glacier repeat photography archives (joint project with George Jr.)
leads_pending_manual_access.contact: Parks Canada national office or Yoho National Park

leads_pending_manual_access.reason: 1906 ACC camp records and participant documentation

note_primary_fonds: See george-vaux/fonds.json for primary detailed record. William S. Vaux Jr. materials are documented as part of the shared Vaux family fonds V653 at Whyte Museum. This record serves as a cross-reference for researchers focusing on William Jr.'s specific contributions to the 1906 expedition and glacier photography.
